$schema_comment: MCP Registry submission metadata for ai-memory v0.7.0. Prepared as part of Task H of audit issue #1153 (NSA CSI MCP Security Audit). Actual submission to the external MCP Registry remains operator-gated — this JSON is the canonical submission payload Jim approves before the external PR is opened.

core.description: Default profile. memory_store / memory_recall / memory_search / memory_list / memory_get / memory_load_family / memory_smart_load plus always-on memory_capabilities bootstrap.

graph.description: Knowledge graph traversal: memory_kg_query / memory_kg_timeline / memory_kg_invalidate / memory_find_paths / memory_get_links plus core.

admin.description: Operator surface: memory_archive_list/restore/purge / memory_pending_approve/reject / memory_consolidate / memory_namespace_set_standard plus core.

power.description: Autonomy + observation: memory_reflect / memory_replay / memory_quota_status / memory_atomise / memory_verify plus core.

full.description: Every advertised tool. 73 callable memory tools + always-on memory_capabilities bootstrap. Asserted by Profile::full().expected_tool_count() == 74 in src/profile.rs.

mcp_metadata.resources: n/a (substrate exposes resources via memory_capabilities resource block per capabilities v3)

frameworks_supported: Claude Code (Anthropic) — SessionStart hook + MCP stdio
Codex CLI (OpenAI) — MCP stdio (Codex config TOML)
Any MCP-speaking client via stdio JSON-RPC 2.0
storage_backends: SQLite (built-in; default; WAL mode; FTS5; HNSW vector index)
PostgreSQL + Apache AGE (--features sal-postgres; AGE Cypher graph backend; auto-detected on pg_extension)

nsa_csi_coverage.reference_document: National Security Agency, Model Context Protocol (MCP): Security Design Considerations for AI-Driven Automation, U/OO/6030316-26 | PP-26-1834, May 2026 v1.0

compliance_evidence.audit_trail_format: V-4 signed_events cross-row hash chain (prev_hash + sequence; schema v34)
compliance_evidence.transport_security: mTLS on federation surface; mTLS optional on HTTP daemon; HMAC-mandatory webhook dispatch under R3-S1.HMAC
compliance_evidence.key_attestation: Ed25519 per-agent keypairs; canonical-bytes signing discipline pinned by regression tests

active_maintenance_proof.commit_cadence: Daily commits on release/* branches per CHANGELOG.md

endorsement_disclaimers.no_anthropic_affiliation: ai-memory is not an Anthropic product. It is an MCP-protocol implementation maintained by AlphaOne LLC under Apache 2.0.
endorsement_disclaimers.no_nsa_endorsement: The NSA CSI mapping (linked above) describes ai-memory's substrate-level posture relative to NSA-issued guidance. The National Security Agency does not endorse, certify, or recommend ai-memory, AgenticMem, AlphaOne LLC, or any commercial product. References to the NSA document follow the document's stated reproduction guidance.

submission_metadata.submission_format_expected: PR to https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/registry or registry submission form (per registry's current submission process; check https://blog.modelcontextprotocol.io/posts/2025-09-08-mcp-registry-preview/ for current process)
submission_metadata.submission_text: ai-memory v0.7.0 — persistent memory substrate for AI agents and LLM tools. 74 advertised MCP tools at --profile full, 7 at --profile core. Schema v49 (sqlite + postgres lockstep). Ed25519 attestation, V-4 signed-events hash chain, 25-event programmable hook pipeline, Form 4/5/6/7 Batman provenance framework, mTLS federation. Procurement-grade NSA CSI MCP security compliance documented at https://alphaonedev.github.io/ai-memory-mcp/compliance/nsa-csi-mcp.html (10/10 concerns + 7/7 recommendations structurally addressed). Apache 2.0. Maintained by AlphaOne LLC.

operator_authorisation_notes: This submission metadata is prepared but NOT yet submitted to the external MCP Registry. Per the discipline of audit issue #1153 Task H, the actual submission requires explicit operator authorisation (Jim) before the external PR is opened against the registry repository. Reason: the registry submission is a public-facing claim with vendor-attribution implications that fall outside autonomous-AI scope. When authorised, the submission text + tags above are the canonical payload; no additional drafting is required.
